asset_name: Warrior unit — Shield brace idle sheet
shared_properties: The warrior occupies a 32x48px footprint with a readable round shield and fur-lined helmet silhouette.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
The pose is authored for side-view battlefield readability against pale snow tiles and dark buildings.
differences: This base sheet covers the neutral guard stance used while the village is waiting for a raid trigger.
The shield angle stays forward and the spear remains low so the unit reads as ready rather than attacking.
A 6-frame loop adds cloak flutter and small stance shifts suited to windy battlefield idles.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the monochrome blizzard mood, Viking settlement silhouettes, and snow density targets.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas-driven battlefield unit sprite spawned as a world entity in defense scenes and result cutaways.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/art_000003.png during the world Loading state and cache the atlas handle for defense encounters.
runtime_notes: Advance the idle loop at 8fps so cloak motion feels alive without looking like an attack wind-up.
Keep feet anchored to the bottom of the 32x48px frame so tile alignment stays stable on the snow grid.
Use whole-pixel translations for unit placement to preserve shield rim clarity.

context.usage: Default defensive warrior state used during raid warnings, village overview scenes, and settlement status screens.

shape.silhouette: Compact Viking guard with round shield forward, low spear, and fur cloak trailing behind the stance.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x48px with 6 frames of 32x48px and 0px spacing.
Shield edge, helmet, and spear tip remain legible against both #FFFFFF snow and #111111 building shadows.
The feet baseline stays aligned across every frame so tile placement remains stable.
